Field agreed and said that she is more focused on looking forward despite naysayers who think that older women can’t land leading parts because they aren’t as compelling.
“I don’t even think about it. I really think about what’s next. And will there be a next, you know?” she replied.
Field continued, “As you get older… boy, (roles are) hard to find. People don’t think older women are interesting, I guess. But I got news for you, it’s all those older women who raised you.”

The veteran actor has tackled a wide range of roles throughout her career, from “Gidget,” to “The Flying Nun,” “Sybil,” “Norma Rae,” “Steel Magnolias,” “Mrs. Doubtfire,” “Forrest Gump,” “Lincoln,” “Brothers & Sisters” and many more.

Speaking about her latest role, Field described her character in “Remarkably Bright Creatures” as “an old woman who has grief in her life” after the death of her husband. “But there’s a deeper grief that she hides and holds, and it has kept her away from the world.”
She told Willie that she and Pullman, whose character also works at the aquarium, ad-libbed some of the scenes where their characters get to know each other.
“We began to improvise, and we just were these people. Just, bam, right out of the chute,” she recalled.
While another, less-experienced actor might feel intimidated to take on a book with a large fanbase, Field said she didn’t feel any pressure and was raring to go when production began.
“So by the time we got to really shoot it… anything that needed to be left, had to be left,” she shared. “Then the allegiance, I feel, has to be in making the film work.”

Field’s passion for acting and taking on different parts hasn’t waned, and she still has goals she wants to achieve. She told Carson Daly her dreams for this stage in her career during an appearance on TODAY May 5.

She shared that she has always known she was born to be an actor. One could say it was in her blood. Field’s mother, Margaret Field, was also an actor and her stepfather, Jock Mahoney, was a stuntman.
“This is what I do. I’m an actor. And I’m proud to be an actor,” she said. “As I get older, and I’m months away from being 80, (finding) the roles about 80-year-old women is my dream. That’s it.”

Before “Remarkably Bright Creatures,” Field’s last film was the 2023 comedy “80 for Brady,” where she, Jane Fonda, Rita Moreno and Lily Tomlin portrayed a group of life-long friends who wanted to go to the Super Bowl and meet Tom Brady.
And born to act she was, telling Willie in their Sunday Sitdown that after the first time she took the stage as a kid, “I left my body. I was doing something but I didn’t tell myself to do it.”
“I heard my voice for the first time,” she said of acting, “and I could be mean, I could be all sorts of things I wasn’t allowed to be.”
When asked if acting was just a natural fit since she discovered it, Field replied, “I guess so... I would’ve been a nurse or ... I don’t know what I would’ve been.”
“Thank goodness for acting I guess, right?” Willie quipped.
“I’ll say,” Field said.

title: Retired Veteran Stunned After Winning Largest ‘The Price Is Right’ Prize In Show’s History
summary: During a special Mother’s Day episode, Vanesa from Virginia won the largest prize ever won in a single pricing game in the show’s 54-year history.
story: This year Mother’s Day will be extra special for one Virginia mother.
Vanesa, a retired veteran, appeared on the May 8 episode of “The Price is Right,” where she won over $240,000 in cash and prizes while playing “The Lion’s Share.”

The new game, which debuted this season as a partnership with BetMGM, has contestants guess the prices of grocery store items to earn up to five balls that have a hidden prize amount. After a contestant chooses a ball, they can either walk away with their current prize or keep playing and risk losing it all.
Vanesa earned five prized circles and had a history-making moment on “The Price Is Right,” taking home the largest prize won in a single pricing game in the show’s 54-year history.
With her daughter supporting her in the crowd, Vanesa began choosing the numbers on the ball.
“This is huge for me,” Vanesa said before first selecting number 13 and instantly nabbing $2,500. She kept going and chose 21, which earned her $25,000.
After the reveal, Vanesa dropped to the floor in disbelief. But her luck didn’t stop there, she chose 25 and won $100,000.

When asked by host Drew Carey if she wanted to continue, Vanesa said the last two numbers were her daughter’s birthday and she needed to keep going. She chose 27 and the number gave her another $100,000.
Left stunned and visibly emotional, with one number left to go, Carey once again offered her two choices: continue playing or end it right there.
“I have to believe,” she said and chose 8, which won her a mother-daughter trip to Morocco worth $12,650. That brought her grand total to $240,150.
“Oh my God,” she repeatedly said, jumping up and down while the camera panned to her daughter, who was also emotional in the audience.
Vanesa’s big win surpassed the previous record of $210,000 in a single pricing game of Cliffhangers that happened in 2016, the show’s press release stated.

title: Rihanna’s New Tattoo Was Drawn By Her Kids and People Have Thoughts
summary: Rihanna’s latest tattoo, “designed by her babies,” drew mixed reactions from fans after the big reveal.
story: Rihanna’s latest tattoo has some parents feeling inspired and others raising eyebrows in disbelief.

Bang Bang Tattoo, a shop in New York City, posted a series of pictures on Instagram May 7 revealing that the singer recently popped in for some more ink. The post teased the new tattoo and also included snaps of owner and artist Keith “Bang Bang” McCurdy posing with Rihanna over the years beside each of the designs he has etched onto her body. The caption said he has been working with Rihanna, whom he referred to as his “sister,” for 20 years.
Fans wondered in the comments what the Grammy winner chose for her new body art. A day later, the speculation was put to rest as the Bang Bang Tattoo account uploaded a slideshow debuting the design.

The first video in the post showed Rihanna filming herself while lying on a table as Bang Bang worked his magic. The second video featured a close-up of the tattoo, located on the back of her knee, which was a replica of a drawing from Rihanna’s three children that she shares with partner A$AP Rocky: son RZA, born in May 2022, son Riot, born in February 2023 and daughter Rocki, born in September 2025.

The design was a series of multi-colored scribbles layered on top of one other. Rihanna’s tattoo re-created the sketch in black. The shop wrote in the caption, “Family tats @badgalriri Designed by her babies,” and included a red heart.

In another video in the post, Rihanna joked, “The torture I’ve endured in this shop.” One photo also showed the “Love on the Brain” singer proudly showing off the ink while standing in front of a mirror.
Some fans left comments praising the mother of three for the sweet gesture honoring her children.
“Imagine looking back at it when they will be older,” one said. “The cutest mom.”
Another wrote, “My nephew drew one of these on a sticky note before he passed away. I think I’m gonna get a tattoo now.”
Others questioned Rihanna’s decision.
“But babe their drawings are only gonna get better,” one pointed out.
One Instagram user simply said, “Well, that’s a choice.”
A third had a suggestion, writing, “Should have done it in the colors … still a lovely memory.”

Rihanna frequently gushes about her children on social media and in interviews. In April, she appeared with Rocki on the cover of W Magazine’s 2026 Pop Issue. The “Diamonds” singer wore a Dior Haute Couture coat paired with a lilac hat and Cartier Collection necklace. Rocki complemented her mom by donning a Dior Haute Couture diaper.
The interview included a quote from A$AP Rocky, who praised his partner.
“She has changed a lot because she became a mother in that time span, and that certainly changes you,” he said about how Rihanna has grown throughout her career.

He added, “But this woman has always been magic. Philosophically, the way she operates is on another level. She is the most charming and genuine person on Earth. Her energy is unmatched — one of a kind. I just adore her.”

title: Ronald McDonald Sings the National Anthem at a Baseball Game — And People Are Lovin’ It
summary: A man dressed as Ronald McDonald has won raves after he sang the national anthem before a Charlotte Knights minor league baseball game at Truist Field.
story: There are critically acclaimed performances. And then there are critically Big Mac-claimed performances.

Minor league baseball is no stranger to unusual gimmicks or attractions, but this one may have taken the cake — or at least the milkshake. A man dressed as Ronald McDonald sang the national anthem before the Charlotte Knights home game against the Gwinnett Stripers on May 1.
It’s unclear what exactly the man’s special sauce was, but his performance was good — like, “I’m so happy the McRib is finally back” good.

It may be the best performance by a singer named Ronald whose last name isn’t Isley or last name McDonald whose first name isn’t Michael, and could be the most ballyhooed and beloved national anthem at a sporting event since Whitney Houston belted it out  before Super Bowl 25 in 1991.

The Knights are the Triple-A affiliate of the Chicago White Sox and  the game was host to a fundraiser for the Ronald McDonald House Charities of Charlotte. The performance has since become a viral sensation.
TODAY.com has reached out to the Knights for comment, but the identity of the singer has not been revealed.
Meanwhile, X users continue to rave.
“When did Ronald switch from cooking burgers to cooking the nationalanthem,” one person wrote.
“Hot take: We should have the National Anthem sung by Ronald McDonald more often,” suggested another.
“There’s a good chance this makes your day better so I recommend watching I laughed out loud,” another person commented.
“that one friend that always has a new job,” McDonald’s wrote.
The video was also shared on the Knights’ Instagram page, where fans gushed there, too.
“I’m lovin’ it,” one person wrote.
“Stop! 🛑 He can dance and sing!?🎤 Who would have thought good ole Ronald had it in him!!!” someone else commented.
“Wait how do we make this happen,” asked the Birmingham Barons, which is the White Sox Double-A team.
It’s not clear who sang the anthem at the next Knights game, but is there a doubt that he or she had some pretty big (clown) shoes to fill?

title: Zara Larsson Pulls a Special Guest Onstage to Dance at TODAY’s Citi Concert Series ... Sheinelle!
summary: Sheinelle Jones is living her best “Lush Life.”The TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle co-host showed off some killer dance moves alongside Zara Larsson as the singer performed live on the TODAY Plaza on May 8.Throughout her “Midnight Sun” tour, Larsson has pulled a fan onstage to perform the song “Lush Life.”
story: Sheinelle Jones is living her best “Lush Life.”
The TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle co-host showed off some killer dance moves alongside Zara Larsson as the singer performed live on the TODAY Plaza on May 8.
Throughout her “Midnight Sun” tour, Larsson has pulled a fan onstage to perform the song “Lush Life.” This time, she welcomed Sheinelle.
Dressed in jeans and one of Larsson’s concert T-shirts, Sheinelle immediately brought the energy and started hyping up the crowd.
When the song’s chorus began, Sheinelle joined Larsson and her dancers and absolutely nailed the choreography.
The happiness Sheinelle exuded during the performance was evident, and many Instagram users applauded her in the comments section when TODAY shared a clip of the dance.
“Love this! She is showing joy!” one wrote.
Another commented, “I am literally obsessed with witnessing @sheinelle_o happiness each day!!!!!!!”
“Sheinelle has got the moves!!! ⭐️” another wrote.

Calling a fan up on stage during “Lush Life” has become a tradition at Larsson’s concerts. Stubhub shared a video of one of the performances where the singer’s guest dancer crushed his vocals and dance moves.
This isn’t the first time Sheinelle has showed off her dance skills on TODAY.
In 2020, she took part in TODAY’s Halloween show and danced to a song from “Chicago” with Dylan Dreyer.
Three years later, Sheinelle fulfilled a dream and performed as a backup dancer for her childhood idol, Janet Jackson, during opening weekend of the singer’s “Together Again” tour.
“It was absolutely magical,” she said of the experience.
In 2025, Sheinelle channeled her inner Beyoncé as she sported a Cowboy Carter costume and busted a move to the singer’s song “Texas Hold ‘Em” during TODAY’s Halloween show.
Afterwards, Sheinelle took to Instagram to express her gratitude for the opportunity to perform a song by one of her favorite singers.

“I’m still processing my day! More ‘thank you’s’ to come, so you can meet the crew behind the scenes who made this happen and my dancers — for now though, I’ll thank you for letting us have fun every year! You guys know … this year has been brutal for me - and I hold two things at once at all times…. which makes this year’s show extra special. It was a dream! Truly,” she wrote.

title: What Does Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni’s Settlement Actually Mean? TODAY’s Legal Expert Explains
summary: A legal dispute between Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni has not appeared to end with them just yet.
story: A legal dispute between Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni has not appeared to end with them just yet.

Three days after the attorneys for the “It Ends With Us” stars announced the two parties had reached a settlement in their long legal saga just weeks before a trial was set to begin, a notice of settlement filed by both parties in federal court in Manhattan on May 7 indicates their disagreement is not fully resolved.
The terms of the settlement were not disclosed. A source familiar with the matter confirmed to NBC News that no money changed hands in the settlement, as TMZ was first to report.
Here’s what to know about the latest legal filing.
Justin Baldoni and Blake Lively Announced  They Settled the Case. So What Is this New Legal Development?

Even after agreeing on a settlement, Lively could potentially end up being awarded an unspecified amount in damages depending on a judge’s ruling in connection with a law passed in California in 2023, according to court documents.
NBC News senior legal correspondent and Weekend TODAY co-anchor Laura Jarrett spoke with TODAY.com about what the latest filing means for both parties.

“So even though both sides have agreed to settle the heart of the matter, which was set to go to trial, Lively’s side says they’re still entitled to damages, and that will be, at least in her view, exclusively decided by the judge,” Jarrett said. “It appears based on court filings that they’ve both agreed to whatever the judge rules and have waived their right to appeal. This is a big deal.”

In the notice of settlement filed on May 7, court documents say “Blake Lively does not release, and retains all claims, rights, and remedies in connection with her pending Motion for Attorneys’ Fees, Treble Damages and Punitive Damages under California Civil Code Section 47.1 in the Wayfarer Action.”

California Civil Code Section 47.1 refers to a law passed in 2023 in the wake of the #MeToo movement that protects plaintiffs who file sexual misconduct claims in good faith. The law is designed to give them legal protection from the common tactic of a retaliatory defamation lawsuit by the person who is accused.

Attorneys for Lively filed a sexual harassment complaint against Baldoni in California Civil Court in December 2024. The complaint alleged that Baldoni repeatedly harassed her on the set of “It Ends With Us” and crossed boundaries during intimate scenes, which he denied. She then filed a federal lawsuit with the same allegations.

In January 2025, Baldoni filed a lawsuit in a U.S. district court accusing Lively, her husband, Ryan Reynolds, Lively’s publicist, Leslie Sloane, and Sloane’s PR firm of defamation and other contractual claims.

Lively’s legal team is now arguing before the court that according to the California law, she should be awarded legal fees, damages and punitive damages because she filed her sexual harassment complaint “without malice” and then Baldoni filed a defamation lawsuit, according to a statement to NBC News by attorneys Michael Gottlieb and Esra Hudson.

“It’s just slightly surprising here because it’s the first indication that she wants to invoke this (California law), and I assume this could’ve been part of the settlement if they bargained for that,” Jarrett said. “Everybody could’ve decided it was better to avoid trial even if the judge decided to award (Lively’s side) some money.”
Will There Be a Trial Over the Latest Accusation by Lively?
No, this dispute will be handled by a judge, who will decide if Lively is entitled to the legal fees and damages or not, according to court documents.

Both parties agreed to “irrevocably waive any appeal of any orders in the Consolidated Action,” according to the notice of settlement filed on May 7. That means they have both agreed to waive their right to appeal, and the judge’s ruling will be final.
There is no specific timetable for when the judge will release his decision.
Both Sides Are Claiming Victory in the Settlement

When the settlement was announced on May 4, the attorneys for both sides released a joint statement that they “acknowledge the process presented challenges and recognize concerns raised by Ms. Lively deserved to be heard.”
However, in the following days, each legal team has released a statement claiming their side won the settlement. Terms of the settlement have not been disclosed.

“This settlement is a resounding victory for Blake Lively,” Gottlieb and Hudson said in a statement to NBC News on May 7. “By agreeing to this settlement, and waiving their right to appeal, Justin Baldoni and every individual defendant now face personal liability for abusing the legal system to silence and intimidate Ms. Lively. And by admitting that Ms. Lively’s concerns ‘deserved to be heard,’ the defendants have ended once and for all the fiction that Ms. Lively ‘fabricated’ claims of sexual harassment and retaliation. From day one Blake Lively’s mission was clear: expose and hold accountable those who weaponize smear campaigns and retaliatory lawsuits to intimidate and silence survivors. That mission continues.”
Baldoni’s attorney, Bryan Freedman, countered with his own statement on May 7.

“Let’s be clear, this is a win and total victory for the Wayfarer parties,” he said. “The court had already dismissed 10 of Ms. Lively’s 13 claims, including every sexual harassment claim, every defamation claim, and all claims against the individual defendants. Ms. Lively voluntarily dismissed the rest. In our view, they settled because they knew they were going to lose in court.”
Freedman also addressed the request by Lively to invoke the California law to award her legal feels and damages.
“All that remains is a pending request for fees based on a very narrow issue that has been with the court since September 2025,” Freedman said in his statement.
What Led Up to the Settlement?
Both sides were dealt blows in rulings by U.S. District Judge Lewis J. Liman before deciding to settle the case instead of going to trial.
In June 2025, Liman dismissed Baldoni’s $400 million countersuit for defamation.
In April 2026, Liman threw out 10 of Lively’s 13 claims against Baldoni, including harassment, defamation and conspiracy.

“I think the difference-maker is a judge dealt Lively a gutting blow and that seems to have tipped the scales to propel them into a settlement,” Jarrett said. “I think it’s directly correlated to that decision last month.”
One of Lively’s attorneys, Sigrid McCawley, released at statement after the bulk of her claims were thrown out by Liman.

“This case has always been and will remain focused on the devastating retaliation and the extraordinary steps the defendants took to destroy Blake Lively’s reputation because she stood up for safety on the set and that is the case that is going to trial,” she said.
Even though Lively’s claims of sexual harassment were thrown out by the judge, it doesn’t preclude her from seeking damages using the California #MeToo law in the latest legal maneuver, Jarrett said.

“It doesn’t seem to require that claims are bought by a jury as long as (Lively) had a reasonable basis to file the sexual harassment claim in the first place,” Jarrett said. “That’s going to be for the judge to determine.”

title: How ‘Grey’s Anatomy’ Said Goodbye to Owen and Teddy in the Season 22 Finale
summary: “Grey’s Anatomy” wrote off Owen and Teddy. Here’s what happened to the two longtime characters and what is next for them.
story: “Grey’s Anatomy” bid farewell to two long-time cast members in the Season 22 finale.
In March, fans of the show learned that Kevin McKidd and Kim Raver, who play Owen and Teddy, would be exiting the ABC medical drama.
McKidd first joined the show’s cast in Season 5. Raver first appeared in Season 6 then left the series after Season 8. She later returned in Season 14.
The Season 22 finale, titled “Bridge Over Troubled Water,” aired on May 7 and wrapped up both characters’ story arcs.
How did the show say goodbye to Owen and Teddy? Here’s a recap of the finale.
Did Owen Survive the Bridge Collapse?
Going into the finale, Owen’s life was at risk after he got caught in his car amid a bridge collapse.
In the finale, Owen manages to escape his car as it is sinking into the water. He then goes on to help first responders save a family of four as they struggle to get out of their own car.
As part of the rescue, Owen has to amputate the leg of a pre-teen girl. He also assists the family’s father, whose chest had been crushed by the car’s steering wheel.

Since Owen regularly drives over the bridge that collapsed, Teddy is concerned. To make matters more distressing, the exes’ last conversation was a fight spurred by Teddy announcing that she got a job in Paris.
After hearing that Owen’s car had been found in the water, Teddy takes comfort knowing that he wasn’t inside the vehicle.
After helping the family of four in the bridge collapse, Owen performs surgery on the father at the hospital. When Teddy walks into the operating room, she sees him and assists with the surgery.
Do Teddy and Owen Get Back Together?
Teddy and Owen have a complicated relationship history. The Army veterans were previously married and divorced this season.
After they finish up in the operating room, the exes decide that their love is worth pursuing. Teddy tells Owen she will turn down the job in Paris and stay with him and their kids.
“I choose us,” she says. “I needed some time to figure out what I wanted for myself, and what I want is you and our kids. I want us,” she said.
Teddy continues, saying, “I have lived all over the world, and the only thing, the only thing that makes me feel like home is you.”
Owen responds by saying that she is going to Paris. Teddy takes this to mean she doesn’t want her to stay with him. What he really means is that he and their children will follow her to Paris.
“I have spent my whole life chasing adventure, but the best thing I’ve ever done is our family,” he says. “I would do anything for the three of you. You’re my life.”
The episode then shows a montage of Teddy and Owen’s journey on “Grey’s Anatomy” and the characters walking away from the hospital together as Owen says, “Let’s go home.”
What McKidd Said About the Ending
In an interview Entertainment Weekly, McKidd reacted to the season finale.

“For a long time, Teddy would always be in the wake of Owen’s decisions and having to react to him and following and chasing him. It was time for Owen to step up and go, ‘I’m going to follow you. I’m going to let you take the lead in this,’” he said. “And as they recommit to each other and realize from this finale episode is that they really can’t live without each other.”

title: The Best Books to Read in May 2026, According to Isaac Fitzgerald
summary: Here are the books Isaac Fitzgerald recommended during a segment on TODAY on May 8, 2026.
story: The weather’s heating up, so you might be treating yourself to one of summer’s best treats: Sitting outside with a book.
If you’re looking for a read, Isaac Fitzgerald is here to help. The author of “American Rambler,” returned to the 3rd Hour of TODAY to offer up a few suggestions.
They include a celebrity memoir by one of today’s most influential writers, a romance and a retelling of a classic tale.
What Isaac’s Reading
"Famesick: A Memoir" by Lena Dunham

What Isaac said: “Famesick is the singular memoir from a singular voice — famously ‘a voice of a generation,’ as the line on her show ‘Girls’ goes — and polarizing figure in millennial culture, Lena Dunham. But you don’t have to be a GIRLS fan to enjoy this marvelous memoir. For starters, it’s rare to get a celebrity memoir this dishy and confessional, this genuinely shocking, sure. But also, you rarely if ever get them this well-written. You can feel how much care and craft went into this book, how much intelligence and skill, which is why it’s getting the response it’s getting, and why it is likely to stick around well past the news cycle.
Best Fantasy
"The Lost Book of Lancelot" by John Glynn

What Isaac said: “This is a wildly entertaining retelling of the King Arthur myth, focusing on the life of Lancelot through a lense of queer exploration. Glynn does a fantastic job blending historical elements alongside adventure and fantasy. Filled with incredible world building, Glynn’s writing shines as he explores the Arthurian era with authority, and breathes new life into these long-known legends.”
Best Nonfiction
"Make Believe" by Mac Barnett

What Isaac said: “Make Believe is a brilliant manifesto by one of our all-time great children’s book authors, Mac Barnett. In this slim volume Mac lays out his argument for children’s literature as a real and important artform, but it is his brilliant conversational writing style and sly, often hilarious stories that carries this book past a mere academic assertion and into the echelons of, well, art.”
Best Romance
"Burnout Summer" by Jenna Ramirez

What Isaac said: “Burnout Summer’ follows the story of Camille Luna, a young woman who has just gotten out of a night in jail for some small crimes after losing her job and spiraling. Camille is picked up by an old friend, Danny, who whisks her off to his beachside hometown in Rhode Island, saving her for the summer from her flailing, corporate life in Washington DC. What ensues is an utterly spectacular will-they-or-won’t-they where the guy is delightfully straightforward and has his life together and the girl is the messy one. Ramirez’s sparkling writing makes the beach town come to life on the page — you can almost smell the sea salt in the air — a perfect blend of escapism and romance.”

title: David Attenborough Turns 100! Watch Him on TODAY — in 1981
summary: As David Attenborough turns 100 on May 8, we’re looking back at his first interview on TODAY in 1981.
story: David Attenborough turned 100 on May 8, and what better time to celebrate the life of the famed historian, writer, environmentalist, documentarian and producer than with his classic TODAY interview from 1981?
Attenborough spoke with Jane Pauley on TODAY to promote his acclaimed 13-part “Life on Earth” series, which examined the evolution of the planet and came out in 1979.
“It’s 3,000, 500 million years of history, of life,” he told Pauley.

“And you may say, ‘How can you imagine that?’ And the answer is, of course, you can’t. So, you may equally say, ‘Well, why bother?’ But it seems to me, at any rate, that the trouble with — not the trouble — but take a long view of the history of life means that you can look past all the details, which are not perhaps significant, and find what is really important about the history of life on Earth.”

As far as man’s impact on the planet, the three-time Primetime Emmy Award winner — who was nominated for best narrator last year for “Asia” — pointed out that humans came to the party relatively late if the world was looked at in terms of an entire year.
“He would’ve turned up just a few minutes before midnight,” he said. “I mean, just the very, very last moments of that year of life, if you condense the whole history into a year.”

Attenborough hasn’t exactly ridden off into a sunset you may see on one of his countless specials, either. Last year, at the age of 99, he set the record for oldest Daytime Emmy winner when he won the award for best outstanding daytime personality — non-daily for his work on the Netflix documentary “Secret Lives of Orangutans,” shattering the mark Dick Van Dyke had set at age 98 for an appearance on “Days of Our Lives.”

Attenborough seems to be basking in the attention that comes with reaching the century mark of his own life. He’s being honored with a live 90-minute special broadcast from the Royal Albert Hall in London.
“I had rather thought that I would celebrate my 100th birthday quietly, but it seems that many of you have had other ideas,” he said, according to the BBC.

“I’ve been completely overwhelmed by birthday greetings, from pre-school groups to care home residents, and countless individuals and families of all ages. I simply can’t reply to each of you separately, but I would like to thank you all most sincerely for your kind messages, and wish those of you who have planned your own local events: Have a very happy day.”

title: Goldie Hawn Reacts to Meryl Streep Saying She Had ‘Beef’ With Her on the Set of ‘Death Becomes Her’
summary: Goldie Hawn shares her thoughts on Meryl Streep saying she was always late on the set of “Death Becomes Her.”
story: Goldie Hawn knows it’s all fun and games when it comes to her friendship with Meryl Streep.

In an interview with Vanity Fair released April 30, Streep, 76, rewatched scenes from her famous films, including 1992’s “Death Becomes Her.” Recalling her time on set with Hawn, the Oscar winner shared that she had “beef” with her co-star for always running late.

“Goldie, she was always late to set,” “The Devil Wears Prada 2” star said. “But she was so adorable. And I’m always on time, you know — and annoying. But she’s late and she had a red convertible, I remember, and she’d drive herself to set. So that was probably the problem.”

“She’d drive herself to set. She had her hair all,” Streep continued, before imitating Hawn, saying, “‘Oh, gosh, sorry!’ And everybody thought, ‘Oh, she’s so cute.’ Yeah. So I had a beef with her. But I loved her. I love her.”
Hawn, 80, was then asked what she thought about Streep’s comment, confirming that she tends to not be on time.
“I think I’m 15 minutes late to everything,” Hawn told “Entertainment Tonight” in an interview shared May 6. “I do. I mean, honestly, it’s unbelievable.”
However, Hawn said, “I think we got through that.”

In “Death Becomes Her,” Streep’s Madeline and Hawn’s Helen go head-to-head, vying for the same man, plastic surgeon Ernest, portrayed by Bruce Willis. The dark comedy, now a cult classic, touches on themes of aging, beauty and the measures one will go to in order to stay forever young.
Hawn added that she and Streep have “been such good friends for so long” and her tardiness is just one of their jokes.
“So she says I was too late on the set,” Hawn continued, quipping, “Maybe, she’s too early?”
“Sometimes when you’re too early, you’re still waiting for somebody and you think, ‘Oh God, where the hell is she?!’” she joked.
In her interview with Vanity Fair, Streep praised her longtime friend by noting that Hawn has “the best laugh in America.”
“She’s one of my buddies and over the years we’ve had some laughs about that movie because people love it,” Streep said. “I thought it was like a documentary on Beverly Hills.”

title: Valerie Bertinelli Says She Hasn’t Had ‘Intimate Love With a Partner’ in ‘Over a Decade’: ‘I Do Miss That’
summary: Valerie Bertinelli opened up about her current love life during an appearance on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle.
story: Valerie Bertinelli has revealed that she hasn’t experienced “intimate love” in “over a decade” despite having recent relationships.

The 66-year-old actor and TV personality appeared on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle on Thursday, May 7, and discussed her new Lifetime movie “Love, Again.” In the film, which premieres May 9 and marks her return to acting, Bertinelli plays a woman whose husband of 30 years is diagnosed with Alzheimer’s disease. As she takes care of him, she forms a friendship and finds a new connection.

Jenna Bush Hager wondered if Bertinelli was inspired by her character and is open to finding love again following her split from her second husband Tom Vitale in 2022 and her divorce from first husband Eddie Van Halen, who died in 2020.

The “Hot in Cleveland” star replied, “I have such exquisite love in my life. I have my animals. I have my girlfriends. I have my son and his beautiful wife. I have my brothers. I have my family. I have beautiful love in my life.”

“But there’s something about intimate love, and I write about it in my book,” she continued, referring to latest book “Getting Naked: The Quiet Work of Becoming Perfectly Imperfect” published in March. “There’s something about an intimate love with a partner, and I haven’t had a partner in over a decade at least.”
She explained that she hasn’t had a recent relationship where she felt like she found a true companion.
“I do miss that. But then there’s another part of this, like, I am so independent and I can do everything on my own,” she added.
Her comments echoed a similar sentiment she shared during an interview with People in March where she said she has “never really experienced” true love.

Bertinelli and Van Halen were married from 1981 to 2007 and had one child together, Wolfgang Van Halen. Then, she was married to Vitale from 2011 to 2022. Following the end of that marriage, she confirmed she was dating writer Mike Goodnough in April 2024. But the two called it quits in November 2024, People reported at the time.
Months later, she penned a lengthy post on Instagram “about mistakes I’ve made in relationships.” She also wrote in the now-deleted post that she “fumbled the last true good man I met.”
During her recent sit-down with People, she reflected on her relationship history.

“Two divorces behind me, I felt like a failure. I didn’t know what love actually meant, really, real true (love),” she shared. “Ed and I had it for glimpses, but I really never really experienced it for any length of time. And I still haven’t. You know, Ed was the closest I’ve ever come to feeling true true love.”

She said she needs a partner who makes her feel safe. In turn, she would like “someone to feel emotionally safe with me, like they can open up and be vulnerable with me because I’ll protect that and I want to feel protected. And I haven’t experienced that either where my vulnerability has been respected and held on to.”
Bertinelli confirmed at the time that she is open to being in another relationship. But if it doesn’t happen, she is content.
“I mean, love, when it’s right, can be magical. And one day I might get that, but I also have to be OK with one day not getting it. So that’s where I’m at. I love my life right now,” she said.

title: Garcelle Beauvais Speaks Out on Fallout From Her ‘RHOBH’ Exit: ‘A Line Was Drawn for Me’
summary: Garcelle Beauvais spoke on TODAY and in her Audible Originals memoir “Protecting My Peace at All Costs” about why she left “Real Housewives of Beverly House.”
story: Garcelle Beauvais is shedding light on her decision to step away from “The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills” in 2025.
The actor, who first joined the Bravo reality series in Season 10, exited the show after filming Season 14.
During the show’s reunion special, Garcelle walked off the set and refused to take a photo with her co-stars, prompting many viewers to wonder what had gone on behind the scenes.
While appearing May 7 on the 3rd Hour of TODAY, Garcelle offered insight into her decision to leave the show.
“A line was drawn for me that I no longer wanted to cross. I felt like my spirit could no longer do it. (It was) just drama for the sake of drama,” she told NBC’s Savannah Sellers.
It’s a topic Garcelle also discusses in her new Audible Original memoir, “Protecting My Peace ... at All Costs,” as she explains how she felt alone while working with the cast.

“I realized something I couldn’t unsee. I didn’t have an ally. Not one. Not a single friend sitting on that stage with me. No one to lock eyes with, no one to lean toward. No one saying, ‘I see you,’” she wrote. “The same way I walked in is how I walked out. Alone. I didn’t wanna fight anymore. I didn’t wanna be a grown a-- woman, a grandmother, going after other women.”
During her 3rd Hour of TODAY interview, Garcelle reiterated this sentiment.

“And like I said (in my book), I felt like there wasn’t anybody there anymore that I connected with. And it was just time. I mean, ‘Housewives’ was never my last stop. I’ve done a lot of things prior to that and hope to do more afterward. But it was just time for me. It really was,” she said.

In her memoir, Garcelle discusses the drama that went on behind the scenes at the Season 14 reunion, including an incident in 2022 when Garcelle’s new book at the time was put in the garbage by her cast mates.

“At the reunion, I found out that there was a group chat that I was never a part of. That’s how my memoir ended up in the trash, posted on social media. And when it all came out, some of the women laughed, like it was a joke about recycling. How do you feel a part of something when there’s a group chat you didn’t even know existed? I thought about leaving then. I thought about leaving after every reunion, if I’m honest,” she wrote.
Going into the reunion, Garcelle intended to take a cast photo, but she changed her mind while filming, saying she felt “a shift” with her co-stars.

“Andy stopped by my dressing room before taping as he always does. He said, ‘How are you feeling?’ I said, ‘Good.’ He said, ‘Really?’ In a way that made me feel like, ‘Oh, s---. Who’s coming for me?’” she wrote.

Garcelle was one of two stars who joined the show’s cast in Season 10. Over time, she developed a friendship with the other new face, Sutton Brown (aka Sutton Stracke). Their bond crumbled amid Garcelle’s exit from the show.
While appearing on “Watch What Happens Live” in October 2025, Garcelle said her friendship with Sutton was over and explained how she believed that Sutton didn’t support her during the reunion.
“Not only she didn’t have my back, she also when she called me, never took accountability for it and that hurt my feelings a lot,” she said.
In the “RHOBH” reunion this year, Sutton expressed hope that she and Garcelle could one day “reconcile or at least talk.”

In response to Garcelle’s claims that Sutton didn’t have her back, Sutton said: “I don’t know why she thinks that, to be honest. I would have done anything for her and (her sons). Anything — and she knows that.”

title: Billie Eilish and Nat Wolff Made Their Red Carpet Debut. Inside Their Relationship History
summary: Billie Eilish and Nat Wolff are red carpet official. Here’s the history of their relationship, how they met and how long they’ve been linked.
story: Billie Eilish and Nat Wolff are red carpet official.
The duo posed together on the red carpet at the premiere of Eilish’s new concert movie, “Billie Eilish — Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour (Live in 3D),” on May 6.

In one photo, the “Birds of a Feather” singer embraced Wolff, an actor and singer who has appeared in several films, including “The Fault in Our Stars” and “Paper Towns.” Wolff got his start in the Nickelodeon show “The Naked Brothers Band,” a mockumentary about his life with his brother.

Eilish, 24, was dressed in a green polo shirt layered over a white blouse and a black pleated skirt. She accessorized with red and blue knee-high socks and silver heels. The musician’s beau, 28, opted for a brown suit and a white shirt.
The two have been linked romantically since last year, with photos published in June 2025 of the two kissing in Venice, Italy.
Most recently, the couple attended the 2026 Grammy Awards together.
Before they were publicly linked, Eilish and Wolff co-starred in the music video for her song “Chihiro” in 2024.
Speaking to Vogue in 2024, Wolff offered the following description of Eilish.

“She’s one of those people where almost everything she does, she does better than everyone else. She’s like, ‘Come see me ride my horse,’ and the teacher says, ‘You know, if she put all her energy into it, she could go to the Olympics,’” he said.
Wolff and his brother, Alex Wolff, served as an opening act for Eilish during her “Hit Me Hard and Soft” tour from 2024 to 2025.

The brothers, in a 2026 interview with Interview magazine, said they learned from Eilish “how to be a world class artist with grace,” all while making fans “feel so special and beloved,” during the tour.
Per Vogue, the two musicians first met at the Academy Museum Gala in Los Angeles and bonded over their shared experience with Tourette syndrome.
While speaking about Eilish, Wolff told Vogue, “You just have to kind of submit to the fact that she’s going to be better than everyone else at everything.”

title: Stanley Tucci Reveals His New TJ Maxx Ad and Opens Up About His Style Evolution
summary: Stanley Tucci opens up about his latest collaboration with TJ Maxx, "The Devil Wears Prada 2" and his favorite fashion purchase of all time.
story: There’s no question that both on-screen and off, Stanley Tucci is a style icon.

Outfitted in his signature glasses and impeccable attire, Tucci is as much known for his dapper fashion as he is for his impressive career, which includes accolades across film, television and Broadway, not to mention a collection of best-selling culinary books.

But it’s style that’s front and center as Tucci returns to the big screen to reprise his role as Nigel Kipling, art director for the fictional magazine Runway in “The Devil Wears Prada 2,” a sequel that’s been 20 years in the making.
“I like the fact that the movie wasn’t made immediately afterwards,” Tucci tells TODAY.com during a phone interview.

“I like the fact that it’s 20 years later, that these people are older and more mature. The world circumstances that we find ourselves in now and the circumstances particularly concerning journalism and AI, the internet... all these changes that are happening now so fast, I think makes it quite an interesting film.”
A lot has changed since the first “Devil Wears Prada” was released back in 2006, including Tucci’s personal style.

“I always liked fashion,” Tucci says. “Before the first film, I always would dress nicely, I always wore a jacket and all that stuff. But after the first film, I became more and more interested in it.”

Citing legendary costume designer Patricia Field as the person who was “instrumental” in helping him unlock the true potential of men’s clothing, Tucci says filming “The Devil Wears Prada” took his fashion game to the next level.
“It opened my mind and that was great,” says Tucci.

Tucci is parlaying that love of fashion into a new ad with TJ Maxx that riffs on the already-classic line from the sequel in which Nigel drolly sniffs, “Look what TJ Maxx dragged in” upon Andy’s (Anne Hathaway) return to the glossy magazine.
“(TJ Maxx) came and they said, ‘Would you be interested in doing an ad?’” Tucci says of the collab. “And the concept was so funny that there was no way I was not going to do it.”
The clever commercial finds Tucci offering a tongue-in-cheek mea culpa for his Nigel’s withering comment saying, “Do you really think that I wrote those words? I’m just a tragically handsome actor.”
He then goes on to admit that far from being like his on-screen counterpart, he’s actually a “Maxxinisto” and, thanks to TJ Maxx, a “very well-dressed” one at that.
“While certain fictional characters may have spoken hastily, I personally have developed a taste for elevated quality without the unnecessary drama of elevated prices,” he sums up in the ad.

While Tucci and Nigel are very different in real life, Tucci clearly shares his character’s love of fine fashion, which begs the question: Is there anything in Tucci’s closet that he considers a serious shopping find?
“I have this jacket. It’s a black leather jacket that is a bit longer,” he says of his score. “It’s really thick and it’s really old.”
According to Tucci, he discovered the one-of-a-kind vintage leather coat in a thrift shop.
“As it turns out, it’s a Chicago policeman’s jacket from the 1940s and it’s made out of horse hide. That’s why it’s so incredibly thick,” explains Tucci.

“I have it in my closet. I seldom wear it, but it’s like, to me, one of the coolest looking ... the leather’s all weathered and messed up, but I can’t get rid of it,” he adds. “I almost want to frame it. You can still see the little shadow or imprint of where the badge was.”
And beyond their mutual love of fashion, Tucci does have some very Nigel-like thoughts on fashion faux pas, including a disdain for athleisure.
“Wear athletic wear when you’re working out – no other time,” Tucci told GQ last month at the premiere of “The Devil Wears Prada 2.”
And he stands by the advice while talking with TODAY.com.
“It’s fine if you’ve just exercised and you have to run to the shop to get something. But, otherwise, no,” says Tucci of the trend of wearing workout clothes in place of more traditional daily wear.
So, is it fair to say that when it comes to good style, fashion wins over comfort?
“Without question,” says Tucci. “It’s not even a question.”

title: Bette Midler, Sarah Jessica Parker and Kathy Najimy to Run Amok in ‘Hocus Pocus 3’
summary: Witches Winifred, Sarah and Mary will again bring chaos and mischief to the world in the upcoming “Hocus Pocus 3.”Bette Midler, Sarah Jessica Parker and Kathy Najimy will reprise their roles as the Sanderson Sisters in the movie, which is in early development at Disney Live Action Studios, TODAY.com has learned.
story: Witches Winifred, Sarah and Mary will again bring chaos and mischief to the world in the upcoming “Hocus Pocus 3.”

Bette Midler, Sarah Jessica Parker and Kathy Najimy will reprise their roles as the Sanderson Sisters in the movie, which is in early development at Disney Live Action Studios, TODAY.com has learned. Deadline first reported the news of the third installment and the possibility that the film will have a theatrical distribution following “Hocus Pocus 2.” The 2022 sequel, also starring Midler, Parker and Najimy, was released on Disney+.

The sisters first wreaked havoc on Salem residents in the original 1993 flick, which has since become a beloved Halloween classic. The movie followed a teenager who accidentally awakened and freed a coven of evil witches who lay dormant in an abandoned house. Nearly 30 years later, another group of naive teenagers brought the witches to life once more.
Fans were delighted to see the trio together again in 2022, and last year the stars gave them hope that another installment was on the way.
In July 2025, Parker appeared on “Watch What Happens Live with Andy Cohen” and revealed that the cast was in talks for “Hocus Pocus 3.”
“No more developments other than we would like to do it,” she said when responding to a fan’s question about the threequel’s status. “We’ve been having some conversations.”

Then a few months later in October 2025, just in time for Halloween, Midler also stopped by Andy Cohen’s talk show and discussed a third film, confirming that a script has already been written thanks to the success of the sequel.
She said “Hocus Pocus 2” amassed “billions of watches” on the streaming platform. “Billions, I’d never heard that number before,” she added.

When asked about the next movie, she said, “They sent a script and a lot of it was brilliant. So I got very excited. And now we’re sort of, like, trying to figure out what it is and where it’s going to be and how much it’s going to cost.”
She said “all those logistical things” were still being decided.

As for Najimy, she gave fans hope that they will see “Hocus Pocus 3” soon when she posted a picture of her Halloween costume on Instagram in October 2025. Najimy dressed as a witch in an ensemble that Mary would surely approve of, donning her twisted wig.
In the comments, the Disney+ official account replied, “Happy Halloween to our Mary Sanderson.”
Midler, Parker and Najimy have yet to comment on the latest news about their next team up.

title: What Jane Fonda and Her ‘Favorite Ex-Husband’ Ted Turner Said About Each Other
summary: Jane Fonda and Ted Turner were married for a decade, sharing words of encouragement, love and support over the years.
story: Jane Fonda and Ted Turner were one of Hollywood’s hottest couples in the 1990s, but the two grew even closer after they divorced.
Fonda, 88, often jokingly referred to the billionaire media mogul and CNN founder, who died May 6 at age 87 following a 2018 diagnosis of Lewy body dementia, as her “favorite ex-husband.”
Shortly after reports of his death, Fonda penned an emotional tribute to Turner of her “immediate thoughts.”

“(Ted) swept into my life, a gloriously handsome, deeply romantic, swashbuckling pirate and I’ve never been the same,” she began in a lengthy Instagram post that included a picture of the former couple embracing. “He needed me. No one had ever let me know they needed me, and this wasn’t your average human being that needed me, this was the creator of CNN, and Turner Classic Movies, who had won the America’s Cup as the world’s greatest sailor. He had a big life, a brilliant mind and a soaring sense of humor.”
Fonda continued to gush about the late mogul, adding that he took care of her and made her believe in herself.

“He gave me confidence. I think I did the same for him, but that’s what women are raised to do. Men like Ted aren’t supposed to express need and vulnerability. That was Ted’s greatest strength, I believe,” she said.
She added that he also taught her about nature, wildlife, business and other subjects thanks to his wide range of knowledge.
Fonda then complimented Turner for being ambitious, noting he was the most competitive person she knew besides Katharine Hepburn.
“Ted was challenging, but I’ve always been up for a challenge, and with Ted it was almost always worth it,” Fonda wrote.
Her tribute continued in the comments. She said she deeply loved Turner and can picture him in heaven surrounded by wildlife. She also mentioned the love she has for his five children.
She concluded, “Rest in Peace, dearest Ted. You are loved and you will be remembered.”
The unlikely couple, who came from opposite sides of the political aisle, were married from 1991 to 2001.

Fonda, an outspoken liberal activist who grew up the daughter of Hollywood legend Henry Fonda, routinely praised Turner, an Atlanta-based businessman who liked to hunt, in interviews for decades after their split.
“He’s outrageous and hysterically funny,” she told People about her ex in 2023.
Fonda, who, like Turner, had been married twice before, also wrote lovingly of the good times the couple shared in her 2005 memoir, “My Life So Far.”

“There were times when something would set us to laughing so hard we’d sink to the floor, like the night when our guffaws collapsed us at the foot of the ‘Gone With the Wind’ staircase at his plantation and we had to crawl up to bed on hands and knees,” she wrote.
Read on to learn more about what Fonda and Turner have said about each other over the years.
Fonda Was Attracted to Turner’s Energy
Shortly after she and Turner began dating in 1990, Fonda told People she was attracted to the maverick businessman's energy.
“Ted can keep up with me,” she said. “He’s a very, very funny, lovable, complicated person.”
The couple wed at Turner’s 8,100-acre plantation, Avalon, in Capps, Florida, according to People.
They split a decade later, in part, because of Turner’s infidelity, Fonda explained in her memoir.
Turner Recalled Crying After Fonda Left Him
In his own 2008 memoir, “Call Me Ted,” Turner recalled the couple's whirlwind courtship, which began in 1990 when Turner asked Fonda for a date after learning of her divorce from activist Tom Hayden.
Fonda declined, but Turner kept insisting. Six months later, sparks flew when the pair finally met up. They married the following year.
Turner also wrote about the devastation he experienced after Fonda left him.
Returning to his Montana ranch for the first time after their split, Turner saw Fonda had removed her belongings.
“Our closets faced each other’s, and when I saw her empty space I sat down on the floor between them and cried,” he wrote.
Turner Called Fonda the Love of His Life
In 2012, CNN’s Piers Morgan asked Turner if Fonda was the “great love of his life.”
“Probably,” Turner responded.
After more prompting from Morgan, Turner revealed he’d never gotten over losing Fonda and he knew he never would.
“When you love somebody and you really love them, you never stop loving them no matter how hard you try, you can’t stop,” Turner explained.
“There’s nothing wrong with that. That’s good,” he added.
In Fonda’s 2018 documentary “Jane Fonda in Five Acts,” Turner described their separation as “very difficult,” adding, “I thought it was a mistake.”
“I’ve survived and so has she, but I feel like I was happier when I was with her than subsequently,” he said.
Fonda Called Turner ‘Sexy’ and ‘Brilliant’ Years After Their Split
In that same documentary, Fonda recalled the painful end of her and Turner’s marriage.
“Ted was a genius... but he was also gorgeous. I mean, he was really a handsome man, and very, very sexy,” Fonda said, sharing that she was attracted to his “wild brilliance.”
But she also decided to give up her career while she was with him and eventually felt like she couldn't be her full authentic self.

“We had a wonderful 10 years, but I had to hide a part of me in order to please him,” she said, noting there was a voice in her head telling her, “‘If you stay, you will never be authentic, you will never be able to be whole.’”
Fonda Spoke Lovingly of Turner Months Before His Death
Months before Turner died, Fonda sang his praises when she spoke at the Georgia Campaign for Adolescent Power and Potential (GCAPP) EmPower Party and Gala in Atlanta on Nov. 13, 2025.
Fonda acknowledged how Turner’s contributions helped her establish the organization in 1995, noting it, “never would have happened if it wasn’t for Ted,” People reported at the time.

“It was a very challenging time in Georgia. Had I not been with Ted, this was something, had he not stood by me with his love and support, we never would have survived,” Fonda said of the businessman, whose public appearances declined after his 2018 diagnosis.
“Ted’s not here,” she added, “but he is here in my heart and I know he is here in a lot of our hearts.”

title: Oust me if you can: Britain’s Starmer challenges mutinous party
summary: LONDON — Take your best shot
story: LONDON — Take your best shot.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
That was British Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s defiant message Tuesday to a growing rebellion within his Labour Party.
More than 80 Labour lawmakers have called for the prime minister to step down or say when he will depart, including several members of his inner government Cabinet.
But in a crunch meeting with ministers inside No. 10 Downing St. early Tuesday, Starmer faced down the growing mutiny.
“The country expects us to get on with governing. That is what I am doing and what we must do,” Starmer said, according to his office.

Starmer is polling as the most unpopular British leader on record, a series of errors and U-turns compounding the sense among many voters that he does not have a plan to fix the country’s stagnant economy. That dismay reached a crescendo last week after Labour was humiliated in the country’s midterms-style elections, with many voters saying they were specifically punishing Starmer himself.

A growing number within his party fear inaction will lead to Labour being smashed by hard-right Reform UK, led by Trump ally Nigel Farage, at the next national election slated for 2029. Yet no clear candidate has emerged to challenge Starmer.
Under chilly blue skies Tuesday, Westminster was rife with speculation about who may attempt to move against their own leader.

title: Trump says ceasefire is ‘on life support’ after rejecting ‘unacceptable’ proposal from Iran
summary: President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran is “on life support” and “unbelievably weak” after the two sides exchanged fire this weekend, with Trump labeling Iran’s peace proposal as “garbage.”
story: President Donald Trump said Monday that the ceasefire with Iran is “on life support” and “unbelievably weak” after the two sides exchanged fire this weekend, with Trump labeling Iran’s peace proposal as “garbage.”
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
The U.S. and Iran have been at an impasse after Tehran earlier Monday dismissed Washington’s own offer as “one-sided” and accused Trump of making “unreasonable,” “excessive demands.”

The rising price of oil — the Brent crude international benchmark ticked up to $104 a barrel at noon — provides a tense backdrop for Trump’s state visit this week to China, a major consumer of energy out of the Persian Gulf.
Trump said he thought a diplomatic solution was still “very possible” because the Iranian leaders “change their mind. They’re very dishonorable people.”

title: Inflation surged to 3.8% in April, its highest level in nearly three years
summary: Inflation surged to 3.8% in April, its highest level in nearly three years, as the war in Iran causes a ripple effect across the economy and energy prices rise.
story: Inflation surged to 3.8% in April, its highest level in nearly three years, according to data released Tuesday, as the war in Iran causes a ripple effect across the economy and energy prices surge.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

As inflation continues to accelerate, it’s eating into Americans’ wages at a rapid clip. April’s inflation rate means that prices are now rising faster than wages, which could exacerbate the affordability crisis that has already been gripping consumers.

The pace of wage gains has been slowing over the past two years. In November, wage growth continued to rise at a pace of almost 4%. April’s jobs report, which was released on Friday, showed that wage growth had slowed to 3.6%.
The overall rise in inflation was in line with what was expected by economists. On a month-over-month basis, inflation rose 0.6%.
Core inflation, which excludes food and energy costs also rose 0.4% from the month prior, the Bureau of Labor Statistics said.  That rise was higher than what had been expected by economists.
As of Tuesday morning, the price of oil had risen more than 70% since the start of the year and the average price of a gallon of gas was $4.50.
“The index for energy rose 3.8 percent in April, accounting for over forty percent of the monthly all items increase,” BLS said in a statement.
Still, energy prices may not have yet fully hit prices.
“Energy costs likely would not start to feed through to core goods prices for at least a few more months,” Citigroup said in a note to clients on Monday.
The BLS also noted that other energy market-linked prices rose in the month, including airfares and apparel. Airfares alone rose 20% from a year ago.
The report also showed that other categories such as “food away from home,” or takeout, prices jumped 0.7% in April, while services, which includes transportation, rose 0.5% from the month before.

title: It started as a joke. Now India’s Diet Coke parties are all the rage as shortages hit shelves.
summary: Love of Diet Coke runs deep for Ishika Gupta, but it was the fear of losing her favorite drink that took the obsession to new heights
story: Love of Diet Coke runs deep for Ishika Gupta, but it was the fear of losing her favorite drink that took the obsession to new heights.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

The closure of the Strait of Hormuz is still rippling across the global economy, and disruption has been particularly acute in parts of Asia, touching everything from gas and energy costs to cooking fuel and fertilizers.
But the shipping logjam in the Persian Gulf is also having unexpected impacts. In India, shops have struggled to keep Diet Coke on the shelves due to a shortage of aluminum cans.

Suppliers told the Reuters news agency that some orders were not being fulfilled due to a can shortage caused by the situation in the Gulf, which accounts for around 9% of global aluminum production. Diet Coke is not sold in plastic bottles in India, unlike most other countries, leaving fans of the drink at risk of losing out.
For Gupta, a 25-year-old marketing and design consultant based in New Delhi, it was an opportunity for fun, so she decided to throw a party celebrating the drink.
“It was a joke,” said Gupta, describing herself as an “avid drinker” of Diet Coke. “I thought only me and two of my friends would show up.”

title: Hantavirus in the U.S.: How easily does the Andes strain spread?
summary: At least 11 passengers from the MV Hondius cruise ship have tested positive for the Andes strain of hantavirus.
story: At least 11 passengers from the MV Hondius cruise ship have tested positive for the Andes strain of hantavirus. Of the 18 Americans who were on board and are now in quarantine facilities in the U.S., at least three are being closely watched for possible infection.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

The latest confirmed cases are all among people who had direct contact with other patients who were on the ship, although concerns about how easily — or not — the Andes strain spreads are growing. Andes is the only type of hantavirus that can pass from person to person.
Three people from the cruise ship have died from the virus.
It has been assumed that the virus is contagious only if someone is in close contact with someone who’s having symptoms. Some experts now suggest it’s possible it may be more contagious than thought.

“What we’re hearing now, including from the doctors who were on the ship, is that at least a few people contracted it without that long, prolonged exposure that we’ve always assumed,” Dr. Ashish Jha, a senior fellow at Harvard University’s Kennedy School, told NBC’s “TODAY” show on Monday.
More on the hantavirus cruise ship outbreak

American passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship arrive back in the U.S. Birders push back on hantavirus fears tied to Argentine city. Covid cruise passengers recall painful memories amid hantavirus outbreak.
American passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship arrive back in the U.S.
Birders push back on hantavirus fears tied to Argentine city.
Covid cruise passengers recall painful memories amid hantavirus outbreak.

Dr. Brendan Jackson, acting director of the high-consequence pathogens and pathology division at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, said in an interview Monday that the close quarters of the cruise ship environment seem to be key in this outbreak.

Cruise ships are notorious for infectious disease outbreaks because they bring together a wide variety of people and stick them in close quarters for days or even weeks — repeatedly sharing high-touch areas like buffets.
“That is definitely different than how we live most of the time,” Jackson said.
Hantavirus is so rare that spread on cruise ships was unheard of until three Hondius passengers were confirmed to have died from the Andes strain this month.
There’s no evidence to suggest the Andes strain has changed to make it more infectious, Jackson said.
While experts maintain that the risk of hantavirus’ spreading widely is extremely low, they say there’s still much to learn about the virus and how it infects people.
How does hantavirus spread from person to person?

Only one of the dozens of hantavirus strains is known to spread among people: the Andes strain. That’s what sickened at least six people who were on the Hondius, and it is suspected in at least two more cases.

The Andes strain doesn’t spread easily, Jackson said. “When it does, it typically involves household members that are doing things like sharing beds or sharing eating utensils or having contact with body fluids,” he said.

Dr. David Fitter, head of the CDC’s Division of Global Migration Health, said at a media briefing Monday at Emory University Hospital in Atlanta, where two passengers are under observation: “This is very different than a respiratory virus. It’s very difficult to spread.”

It’s also possible that the virus can spread between people if they’re physically close to one another for an extended period of time. The CDC tends to define that as being within 6 feet of another person for longer than 15 minutes, Jackson said earlier Monday at a media briefing at the Nebraska Biocontainment Unit in Omaha, where most of the U.S. passengers from the ship were taken for observation.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google

“There’s nothing magical about 6 feet. It’s not a force field, but it’s a rough number that gives us a sense of how close somebody has been,” he said. “It’s a fairly conservative approach given the evidence we have so far about how this virus has spread in the past.”

Most people with hantavirus, including the Andes strain, become infected after having breathed in or otherwise ingested particles from the urine or droppings from rodents that carry the virus. Just 2% to 5% of all Andes cases are estimated to stem from person-to-person transmission.

Large outbreaks are extremely rare. There was one documented “superspreader” outbreak in Argentina in 2018. Thirty-four people were sickened after several people with hantavirus attended a variety of social events, including a birthday party. Eleven people died.
Can infected people spread hantavirus before they have symptoms?
Jackson said there’s no documented evidence that infected people can spread the virus if they don’t have any symptoms, like coughing or sneezing.
That doesn’t mean asymptomatic spread is impossible.

Dr. Rachael Lee, chief health care epidemiologist at the University of Alabama at Birmingham, said the problem with hantavirus is that patients are identified only when they’re sick enough to be hospitalized. “What we don’t have good data on is how many people are potentially exposed and then just develop antibodies,” Lee said. “We don’t really know too much about transmission from one person to the next.”

Hantavirus is unlike more infectious viruses, like the flu and Covid, because it tends to settle deeper in the lungs, rather than in the upper airways, said Dr. Nicole Iovine, the chief epidemiologist and an infectious disease expert at the University of Florida Health’s Shands Hospital.
“That’s one of the things that would limit the spread of the Andes virus,” she said, “because it’s not in your throat or your nose.”
It’s also why hantavirus can be dangerous, with a fatality rate of up to 50%. The virus can inflame blood vessels, making it difficult for a person to get enough oxygen.
Jackson of the CDC acknowledged that there’s a lot more to learn about hantavirus.
“It’s important to remember that we’re very early in this outbreak,” he told NBC News. “The risk to the public is very, very low at this time, but that doesn’t mean we’re letting our guard down.”

title: U.S. and China arrest 5 in joint drug smuggling investigation ahead of Trump trip
summary: U.S. and Chinese authorities jointly arrested five suspects in a cross-border smuggling and trafficking operation, Beijing said Monday, touting the cooperation ahead of President Donald Trump’s state visit this week.
story: HONG KONG — U.S. and Chinese authorities jointly arrested five suspects in a cross-border smuggling and trafficking operation, Beijing said Monday, touting the cooperation ahead of President Donald Trump’s state visit this week.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

Three Americans and two Chinese nationals were arrested in a joint operation of the Drug Enforcement Administration and the narcotics control bureau of China’s Public Security Ministry that spanned Florida, Nevada and the Chinese provinces of Liaoning and Guangdong, state broadcaster CCTV said.
Authorities also seized drugs, including protonitazene and bromazolam, it added.

The Justice Department said in a statement Monday that a Chinese national and a Las Vegas man had been indicted “for their roles in a scheme to import large quantities of the powerful synthetic opioid protonitazene in the U.S. and distribute it as counterfeit pills.”

Special Agent in Charge David L. King of the Drug Enforcement Administration Asia Pacific Division commended the Chinese Ministry of Public Security “for their thorough and swift investigative work on this case” in the statement.

Other agents also praised the cooperation. “This was the very definition of agents and officers working in unison to bring these criminals to justice,” said Special Agent in Charge Miles Aley of the DEA, Miami Field Division in the statement. “Lives will be saved because of their efforts.”

The announcement came just days ahead of a high-stakes summit between Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing, where reducing the flow of drugs, including fentanyl, is expected to be high on the agenda.

title: Americans from hantavirus-hit cruise ship being monitored for observation, U.S. officials say
summary: The 18 Americans who were aboard the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship Hondius arrived back in the U.S. on Monday, and two of them are in biocontainment units in Omaha, Nebraska, and Atlanta
story: The 18 Americans who were aboard the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship Hondius arrived back in the U.S. on Monday, and two of them are in biocontainment units in Omaha, Nebraska, and Atlanta.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
Those two Americans traveled in the plane’s biocontainment units “out of an abundance of caution,” the Department of Health and Human Services said in a statement.
Most of the Americans were taken to a facility in Nebraska, but two, a passenger on the ship and a close contact, were taken to Emory University Hospital in Atlanta, officials said.
Officials at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and at Emory said Monday that the system was working as intended.
“We have been preparing for years for viruses such as Andes viruses,” said Dr. Aneesh Mehta, chief of infectious disease services at Emory University Hospital.
Since the ship, the MV Hondius, departed Argentina on April 1, there have been seven confirmed cases of hantavirus and three others identified as possible. Three of the 10 people have died.

The luxury cruise ship arrived early Sunday at the island of Tenerife, the largest of Spain’s Canary Islands off West Africa, where passengers started repatriating to their home countries. Passengers received health screenings upon arrival in Spain, the country’s Health Ministry said in a statement.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google
A little more than two dozen crew members remained on board Monday to continue to the Netherlands.

“Let me be crystal clear: the risk of hantavirus to the general public remains very, very low. The Andes variant of this virus does not spread easily, and it requires prolonged close contact with someone who is already symptomatic,” said Dr. Brian Christine, DHS’ assistant secretary for health.
Americans treated in Omaha and Atlanta

U.S. health officials said 16 of the Americans would be treated at the Administration for Strategic Preparedness and Response Regional Emerging Special Pathogen Treatment Center at the University of Nebraska Medical Center/Nebraska Medicine (UNMC) in Omaha.

The two other passengers were taken to Emory University’s Serious Communicable Diseases unit in Atlanta, with one receiving care in the biocontainment unit for mild symptoms. The other passenger is the person’s significant other and is being monitored because they were in close contact with the symptomatic person, Emory University said.
The American passengers range in age from the late 20s to the early 80s.

Two are from New York state — including one from New York City — one is from North Carolina, and two are from California, officials said. Two other Californians are being monitored in that state after either having been on the ship or on a plane with an ill passenger, the state health officer said.

Dr. Brendan Jackson, the CDC’s acting director of high-consequence pathogens and pathology, said at a news conference Monday that it is relatively normal for people in quarantine to show symptoms during isolation and that officials are being “very liberal” with how they are describing symptoms.
Having symptoms does not necessarily mean a patient has contracted hantavirus, Jackson said. Hantavirus usually requires very close contact and symptoms for a patient to pass it on.
Jackson said the decision to send two passengers to Atlanta was “contingency planning” to keep spaces open at Omaha’s UNMC should they need more room.
At UNMC, 15 patients are in the quarantine unit, and one is in the biocontainment unit, said Dr. Michael Wadman, medical director of the National Quarantine Unit.

Dr. Angela Hewlitt, medical director of the Nebraska Biocontainment Unit, said Monday that it is being used for patients who are well but need to be monitored. She described it as “more like a hotel than a patient care space.”

In the unit, which is equipped like an intensive care unit, patients can range from well and stable to critically ill. The one Hondius passenger in biocontainment at UNMC is doing well and does not have symptoms, Hewlitt said.

Hondius passengers will stay at UNMC for a few days for assessments and next steps, but a full 42-day monitoring period will be required for all of those who were on the ship, Jackson said. Patients may be able to go home during that period, but only if they have support to isolate at home.

One traveler, Jake Rosmarin, posted from his quarantine room “to let everyone know I’m okay and feeling well.” He wrote on Instagram that the “repatriation flight was smooth” and that he arrived safely in Nebraska. A photo of his room showed that it has no windows but appeared to have a spin bike and a smart TV.

“It’s been a very long few days, but hopefully I can start giving more updates again soon,” Rosmarin wrote. “Thank you again to everyone who has been supportive throughout all of this; it truly means a lot.”
In a video shared Monday morning, Jan Dobrogowski, the captain of the Hondius, thanked passengers and crew members, acknowledging the difficult weeks they have faced on board.

“At sea, people depend on each other,” Dobrogowski said. “There’s no readily available risk services standing by to come to your house in an emergency, so perseverance is an expected quality, perhaps; but I’ve seen, I’ve witnessed, way more, way more this time around.”
Dobrogowski took time to thank the crew for their “courage and their selfless resolve that they showed time and again in the most difficult moments.”
He expressed condolences for those whose lives were lost and asked for privacy for himself, his crew and the guests on the voyage.
Other passengers being evacuated
The Dutch-flagged Hondius departed Argentina on April 1 with almost 150 on board on a nature sightseeing mission via some of the world’s most remote points.

A Dutch passenger died at sea 11 days later. His body was taken off the ship as it stopped at the remote Atlantic island of St. Helena. The passenger’s wife traveled from there to Johannesburg, South Africa, where she died in a hospital days later.

A World Health Organization investigation is underway to pinpoint the origin of this outbreak, with particular attention placed on a bird-watching trip in southern Argentina, which the first passenger to die took part in before joining the cruise.
American and global health officials have stressed throughout the outbreak that the risk to the wider public is low and that transmission is limited to close contact.

All passengers and a “limited” number of crewmembers had disembarked from the Hondius by Monday, and the ship, with 27 crewmembers remaining on board, is sailing to Rotterdam in the Netherlands, Oceanwide Expeditions said. It is scheduled to arrive there May 17, it said.

A woman who was among five French passengers repatriated Sunday to Paris has tested positive for hantavirus, and her health worsened in the hospital overnight, French Health Minister Stephanie Rist said Monday.

One Spanish national who was on the cruise ship and disembarked Sunday has tested preliminarily positive, the Spanish Health Ministry said Monday. That person has no symptoms and is in good condition, it said.

The U.K. government said 20 British nationals, a German citizen who is a U.K. resident and a Japanese passenger were being kept in a hospital in northwest England for 72 hours and will be asked to isolate at home for 45 days. None are showing any symptoms.

The Swiss Federal Office of Public Health said that one person is being treated after testing positive and that the person’s wife is self-isolating as a precaution. In addition, a Swiss crewmember is also in quarantine in the Netherlands, and another Swiss national is self-isolating in Switzerland.

Tests have confirmed that two of the three people who died had hantavirus. In the case of the first person who died onboard on April 11, no microbiological tests were performed, and his is considered a probable case, the WHO has said.

title: U.S. Coast Guard seizes boat of husband whose wife disappeared in Bahamas
summary: The U.S. Coast Guard has taken possession of the sailboat owned by the American man whose wife disappeared in the Bahamas last month, an official said.
story: The U.S. Coast Guard has taken possession of the sailboat owned by the American man whose wife disappeared in the Bahamas last month, an official said Monday.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

The official, from the Coast Guard’s station in Fort Pierce, Florida, referred additional questions about the Soulmate to the military branch’s Miami office, which did not immediately respond to a request for comment Monday night.
A Michigan-based attorney for Brian Hooker did not immediately respond to requests for comment. Hooker could not be reached for comment.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google
Hooker told authorities in the Bahamas that his wife, Lynette Hooker, vanished after she fell from the couple’s dinghy on the evening of April 4 while they were traveling from Hope Town to Elbow Cay.
